338	fn expand_cuda_module(module: ItemMod) -> syn::Result<TokenStream2> {
339	    let module_attrs = &module.attrs;
340	    let vis = &module.vis;
341	    let ident = &module.ident;
342	    let Some((_brace, items)) = &module.content else {
343	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
344	            &module.ident,
345	            "cuda_module requires an inline module so kernel signatures are visible",
346	        ));
347	    };
348	
349	    let kernels = collect_cuda_module_kernels(items)?;
350	    if kernels.is_empty() {
351	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
352	            &module.ident,
353	            "cuda_module found no #[kernel] functions in this module",
354	        ));
355	    }
356	    let constants = collect_cuda_module_constants(items, ident)?;
357	    let module_items = cuda_module_items_with_constant_symbols(items, &constants);
358	
359	    let non_generic_kernels = kernels.iter().filter(|kernel| !kernel.is_generic);
360	    let function_fields = non_generic_kernels.clone().map(|kernel| {
361	        let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
362	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
363	        quote! {
364	            #(#cfg_attrs)*
365	            #field: ::cuda_core::CudaFunction,
366	        }
367	    });
368	
369	    let function_initializers = non_generic_kernels.map(|kernel| {
370	        let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
371	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
372	        let marker = cuda_kernel_marker_name(&kernel.fn_name);
373	        quote! {
374	            #(#cfg_attrs)*
375	            #field: module.load_function(<#marker as ::cuda_host::CudaKernel>::PTX_NAME)?,
376	        }
377	    });
378	
379	    let artifact_anchor_statements = cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements(&kernels)?;
380	    let has_generic = kernels.iter().any(|k| k.is_generic);
381	    let module_loader = if has_generic {
382	        // At least one kernel is generic: its PTX is emitted into the
383	        // consuming binary's bundle, not this crate's bundle. Merge all
384	        // PTX bundles from the executable so generic monomorphizations are
385	        // visible regardless of which crate compiled them.
386	        quote! {
387	            let _ = name; // merged load ignores the crate-name hint
388	            let module = ::cuda_host::load_all_ptx_bundles_merged(ctx)?;
389	        }
390	    } else {
391	        quote! {
392	            let module = ::cuda_host::load_embedded_module(ctx, name)?;
393	        }
394	    };
395	    let constant_fields = constants.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_constant_field);
396	    let constant_initializers = constants
397	        .iter()
398	        .map(generate_cuda_module_constant_initializer);
399	    let launch_methods = kernels.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_launch_method);
400	    let constant_resolver_methods = constants
401	        .iter()
402	        .map(generate_cuda_module_constant_resolver_method);
403	    let set_constant_methods = constants
404	        .iter()
405	        .map(generate_cuda_module_set_constant_method);
406	    let async_module_items = if cfg!(feature = "async") {
407	        quote! {
408	            pub fn load_async(
409	                device_id: usize,
410	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::cuda_async::error::DeviceError> {
411	                load_async_named(device_id, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
412	            }
413	
414	            pub fn load_async_named(
415	                device_id: usize,
416	                name: &str,
417	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::cuda_async::error::DeviceError> {
418	                ::cuda_host::load_cuda_module_from_async_context(device_id, |ctx| load_named(ctx, name))
419	            }
420	        }
421	    } else {
422	        TokenStream2::new()
423	    };
424	    let async_launch_methods = if cfg!(feature = "async") {
425	        let async_launch_methods = kernels.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_async_launch_method);
426	        let owned_async_launch_methods = kernels
427	            .iter()
428	            .map(generate_cuda_module_owned_async_launch_method);
429	        quote! {
430	            #(#async_launch_methods)*
431	            #(#owned_async_launch_methods)*
432	        }
433	    } else {
434	        TokenStream2::new()
435	    };
436	
437	    Ok(quote! {
438	        #(#module_attrs)*
439	        #vis mod #ident {
440	            #(#module_items)*
441	
442	            #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
443	            #[allow(non_snake_case)]
444	            pub struct LoadedModule {
445	                __module: ::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule>,
446	                __generic_functions: ::std::sync::Arc<
447	                    ::std::sync::Mutex<
448	                        ::std::collections::HashMap<&'static str, ::cuda_core::CudaFunction>
449	                    >
450	                >,
451	                #(#function_fields)*
452	                #(#constant_fields)*
453	            }
454	
455	            pub fn load(
456	                ctx: &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaContext>,
457	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError> {
458	                load_named(ctx, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
459	            }
460	
461	            pub fn load_named(
462	                ctx: &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaContext>,
463	                name: &str,
464	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError> {
465	                #artifact_anchor_statements
466	                #module_loader
467	                from_module(module).map_err(::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError::Driver)
468	            }
469	
470	            pub fn from_module(
471	                module: ::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule>,
472	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
473	                Ok(LoadedModule {
474	                    __module: module.clone(),
475	                    __generic_functions: ::std::sync::Arc::new(
476	                        ::std::sync::Mutex::new(::std::collections::HashMap::new())
477	                    ),
478	                    #(#function_initializers)*
479	                    #(#constant_initializers)*
480	                })
481	            }
482	
483	            #async_module_items
484	
485	            impl LoadedModule {
486	                pub fn as_cuda_module(&self) -> &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule> {
487	                    &self.__module
488	                }
489	
490	                #(#launch_methods)*
491	                #(#constant_resolver_methods)*
492	                #(#set_constant_methods)*
493	                #async_launch_methods
494	            }
495	        }
496	    })
497	}
498	
499	fn collect_cuda_module_kernels(items: &[Item]) -> syn::Result<Vec<CudaModuleKernel>> {
500	    let mut kernels = Vec::new();
501	    for item in items {
502	        let Item::Fn(item_fn) = item else {
503	            continue;
504	        };
505	        if !has_attr_named(&item_fn.attrs, "kernel") {
506	            continue;
507	        }
508	        if let Some(err) = impl_trait_parameter_error(item_fn, "kernel") {
509	            return Err(err);
510	        }
511	        let cluster_dim = cuda_module_cluster_dim(&item_fn.attrs)?;
512	        let cooperative = cuda_module_cooperative(&item_fn.attrs)?;
513	        let params = cuda_module_params(item_fn)?;
514	        let is_generic = has_codegen_generics(&item_fn.sig.generics);
515	        kernels.push(CudaModuleKernel {
516	            vis: item_fn.vis.clone(),
517	            cfg_attrs: cuda_module_cfg_attrs(&item_fn.attrs),
518	            method_attrs: cuda_module_method_attrs(&item_fn.attrs),
519	            unsafety: item_fn.sig.unsafety,
520	            fn_name: item_fn.sig.ident.clone(),
521	            generics: item_fn.sig.generics.clone(),
522	            params,
523	            cluster_dim,
524	            cooperative,
525	            is_generic,
526	        });
527	    }
528	    Ok(kernels)
529	}
530	
531	/// Generate the statements that pin this crate's embedded device artifact
532	/// into the final binary.
533	///
534	/// The codegen backend stores each crate's compiled device code (PTX,
535	/// cubin, NVVM IR, or LTOIR) in a `.oxart` data section of a small extra
536	/// object file. When the crate that holds the `#[cuda_module]` is a
537	/// *library*, that object becomes one member of the crate's `.rlib`
538	/// archive, and linkers only extract an archive member when it defines a
539	/// symbol that some already-linked object references. The backend defines
540	/// a global anchor symbol inside the artifact object for exactly this
541	/// purpose; here we emit the matching reference. Reading the anchor's
542	/// address through `black_box` inside `load_named()` means that any
543	/// program calling `load()` carries an undefined reference to the anchor,
544	/// which forces the linker to pull the artifact member out of the rlib.
545	/// Without this handshake the bundle was silently dropped and `load()`
546	/// failed at runtime with `ModuleNotFound` (issue #72).
547	///
548	/// Without an owner filter, both sides keep using the legacy package+version
549	/// anchor for compatibility with older wrappers and backends. A non-empty
550	/// owner filter activates the v2 package+version+crate+binary identity. That
551	/// target-specific identity prevents an unselected binary from satisfying a
552	/// selected library's reference (or vice versa); an unselected new macro emits
553	/// no reference at all. The backend also keeps a weak legacy alias for older
554	/// macro expansions in mixed-version builds.
555	///
556	/// The reference is only emitted when the module is guaranteed to produce
557	/// an artifact for this crate. Generic kernels are monomorphized (and
558	/// their PTX embedded) in the *consuming* crate, so a module with only
559	/// generic kernels yields no artifact here, and an anchor reference would
560	/// be an undefined-symbol link error. The same reasoning extends to
561	/// `#[cfg]`-gated kernels: the anchor is guarded by the disjunction of
562	/// the kernels' cfg conditions so it is only referenced when at least one
563	/// concrete kernel is actually compiled.
564	fn cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements(
565	    kernels: &[CudaModuleKernel],
566	) -> syn::Result<TokenStream2> {
567	    let (Ok(package_name), Ok(package_version), Ok(crate_name)) = (
568	        std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
569	        std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
570	        std::env::var("CARGO_CRATE_NAME"),
571	    ) else {
572	        // Not built by cargo (e.g. a raw rustc invocation): the backend
573	        // falls back to crate-name-based bundle naming and we cannot
574	        // reproduce it exactly, so skip the anchor rather than risk an
575	        // undefined symbol.
576	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
577	    };
578	
579	    let owner_filter = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE").ok();
580	    let owner_selection = device_codegen_owner_selection(owner_filter.as_deref(), &crate_name);
581	    if owner_selection == Some(false) {
582	        // The backend deliberately omits this crate's artifact. Omitting the
583	        // reference as well keeps the host link valid without pretending that
584	        // a loadable bundle exists.
585	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
586	    }
587	
588	    let non_generic: Vec<&CudaModuleKernel> =
589	        kernels.iter().filter(|kernel| !kernel.is_generic).collect();
590	    if non_generic.is_empty() {
591	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
592	    }
593	
594	    let cfg_guard = if non_generic.iter().any(|kernel| kernel.cfg_attrs.is_empty()) {
595	        // At least one concrete kernel is compiled unconditionally, so the
596	        // artifact object always exists: no guard needed.
597	        None
598	    } else {
599	        // Every concrete kernel is cfg-gated. Reference the anchor only
600	        // when at least one of them is enabled. A kernel with several cfg
601	        // attributes requires all of them, hence all(...) per kernel
602	        // joined under any(...).
603	        let alternatives = non_generic
604	            .iter()
605	            .map(|kernel| {
606	                let predicates = kernel
607	                    .cfg_attrs
608	                    .iter()
609	                    .map(|attr| attr.parse_args::<TokenStream2>())
610	                    .collect::<syn::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
611	                Ok(quote! { all( #(#predicates),* ) })
612	            })
613	            .collect::<syn::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
614	        Some(quote! { #[cfg(any( #(#alternatives),* ))] })
615	    };
616	
617	    let binary_name = std::env::var("CARGO_BIN_NAME").ok();
618	    let anchor = if owner_selection.is_some() {
619	        artifact_anchor_symbol_v2(
620	            &package_name,
621	            &package_version,
622	            &crate_name,
623	            binary_name.as_deref(),
624	        )
625	    } else {
626	        artifact_anchor_symbol(&package_name, &package_version)
627	    };
628	    let anchor_name = LitStr::new(&anchor, proc_macro2::Span::call_site());
629	    Ok(quote! {
630	        // Keep-alive handshake with the codegen backend: see the macro
631	        // crate's `cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements` for details.
632	        #cfg_guard
633	        let _artifact_anchor: *const ::core::primitive::u8 = {
634	            unsafe extern "C" {
635	                #[link_name = #anchor_name]
636	                static CUDA_OXIDE_BUNDLE_ANCHOR: ::core::primitive::u8;
637	            }
638	            ::std::hint::black_box(unsafe {
639	                ::core::ptr::addr_of!(CUDA_OXIDE_BUNDLE_ANCHOR)
640	            })
641	        };
642	    })
643	}
644	
645	fn device_codegen_owner_selection(raw: Option<&str>, crate_name: &str) -> Option<bool> {
646	    let crate_name = crate_name.trim().replace('-', "_");
647	    raw.and_then(|raw| {
648	        let owners: Vec<_> = raw
649	            .split(',')
650	            .map(|name| name.trim().replace('-', "_"))
651	            .filter(|name| !name.is_empty())
652	            .collect();
653	        (!owners.is_empty()).then(|| owners.iter().any(|owner| owner == &crate_name))
654	    })
655	}
656	
657	/// A `#[constant]` static collected from a `#[cuda_module]` body.
658	struct CudaModuleConstant {
659	    ident: Ident,
660	    ty: Box<Type>,
661	    cfg_attrs: Vec<syn::Attribute>,
662	    method_attrs: Vec<syn::Attribute>,
663	    symbol: String,
664	}
665	
666	fn collect_cuda_module_constants(
667	    items: &[Item],
668	    module_ident: &Ident,
669	) -> syn::Result<Vec<CudaModuleConstant>> {
670	    let mut constants = Vec::new();
671	    for item in items {
672	        let Item::Static(item_static) = item else {
673	            continue;
674	        };
675	        if !has_attr_named(&item_static.attrs, "constant") {
676	            continue;
677	        }
678	        if extract_constant_inner_ty(&item_static.ty).is_none() {
679	            return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
680	                &item_static.ty,
681	                concat!(
682	                    "#[constant] static must have type `ConstantMemory<T>` ",
683	                    "(e.g. `static FOO: ConstantMemory<[f32; 4]> = ConstantMemory::UNINIT;`). ",
684	                    "The wrapper prevents the compiler from constant-folding the initializer into kernel bodies.",
685	                ),
686	            ));
687	        }
688	        constants.push(CudaModuleConstant {
689	            ident: item_static.ident.clone(),
690	            ty: item_static.ty.clone(),
691	            cfg_attrs: cuda_module_cfg_attrs(&item_static.attrs),
692	            method_attrs: cuda_module_method_attrs(&item_static.attrs),
693	            symbol: cuda_module_constant_symbol(module_ident, &item_static.ident),
694	        });
695	    }
696	    Ok(constants)
697	}
698	
699	fn cuda_module_constant_symbol(module_ident: &Ident, ident: &Ident) -> String {
700	    let start = ident.span().start();
701	    let base = format!(
702	        "{}_L{}C{}_{}",
703	        module_ident, start.line, start.column, ident
704	    );
705	    constant_symbol(&base)
706	}
707	
708	fn cuda_module_items_with_constant_symbols(
709	    items: &[Item],
710	    constants: &[CudaModuleConstant],
711	) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
712	    let mut constants = constants.iter();
713	    items
714	        .iter()
715	        .map(|item| {
716	            let Item::Static(item_static) = item else {
717	                return quote! { #item };
718	            };
719	            if !has_attr_named(&item_static.attrs, "constant") {
720	                return quote! { #item };
721	            }
722	            let constant = constants
723	                .next()
724	                .expect("constant collection and rewrite order drifted");
725	
726	            let mut item_static = item_static.clone();
727	            let symbol = LitStr::new(&constant.symbol, constant.ident.span());
728	            item_static.attrs = item_static
729	                .attrs
730	                .into_iter()
731	                .map(|attr| {
732	                    if attr_path_ends_with(&attr, "constant") {
733	                        let path = attr.path().clone();
734	                        parse_quote!(#[#path(export_name = #symbol)])
735	                    } else {
736	                        attr
737	                    }
738	                })
739	                .collect();
740	            quote! { #item_static }
741	        })
742	        .collect()
743	}
744	
745	fn cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident: &Ident) -> Ident {
746	    format_ident!("__{}", ident)
747	}
748	
749	fn cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident: &Ident) -> Ident {
750	    format_ident!("__resolve_{}", ident)
751	}
752	
753	/// Extract `T` from a `ConstantMemory<T>` type path. Returns `None` for anything
754	/// that's not a path ending in `ConstantMemory<...>` with one generic argument.
755	fn extract_constant_inner_ty(ty: &Type) -> Option<&Type> {
756	    let Type::Path(type_path) = ty else {
757	        return None;
758	    };
759	    let last = type_path.path.segments.last()?;
760	    if last.ident != "ConstantMemory" {
761	        return None;
762	    }
763	    let syn::PathArguments::AngleBracketed(args) = &last.arguments else {
764	        return None;
765	    };
766	    if args.args.len() != 1 {
767	        return None;
768	    }
769	    args.args.iter().find_map(|a| match a {
770	        syn::GenericArgument::Type(t) => Some(t),
771	        _ => None,
772	    })
773	}
774	
775	/// Like [`extract_constant_inner_ty`] but for sites that have already been
776	/// gated by `#[constant]`'s type-path validation, so extraction failure is
777	/// a compiler-internal invariant violation, not a user error.
778	fn constant_inner_ty(ty: &Type) -> &Type {
779	    extract_constant_inner_ty(ty).unwrap_or_else(|| {
780	        panic!(
781	            "#[cuda_module] collected a #[constant] static whose type is not ConstantMemory<T>; \
782	             this should have been rejected by the #[constant] attribute"
783	        )
784	    })
785	}
786	
787	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_field(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
788	    let CudaModuleConstant {
789	        ident, cfg_attrs, ..
790	    } = constant;
791	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
792	    quote! {
793	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
794	        #field: ::std::sync::Arc<::std::sync::Mutex<::core::option::Option<::cuda_core::ConstantHandle>>>,
795	    }
796	}
797	
798	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_initializer(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
799	    let CudaModuleConstant {
800	        ident, cfg_attrs, ..
801	    } = constant;
802	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
803	    quote! {
804	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
805	        #field: ::std::sync::Arc::new(::std::sync::Mutex::new(::core::option::Option::None)),
806	    }
807	}
808	
809	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_resolver_method(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
810	    let CudaModuleConstant {
811	        ident,
812	        ty,
813	        cfg_attrs,
814	        symbol,
815	        ..
816	    } = constant;
817	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
818	    let resolver = cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident);
819	    let symbol_lit = LitStr::new(symbol, ident.span());
820	    let inner_ty = constant_inner_ty(ty);
821	    let mismatch_msg = format!(
822	        "host/device size mismatch for #[constant] static `{ident}`: \
823	         device size {{}} bytes, host expected {{}} bytes (PTX symbol `{symbol}`)"
824	    );
825	    quote! {
826	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
827	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
828	        fn #resolver(&self) -> ::core::result::Result<::cuda_core::ConstantHandle, ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
829	            let mut slot = self
830	                .#field
831	                .lock()
832	                .expect("cuda constant handle cache mutex poisoned");
833	            if let ::core::option::Option::Some(handle) = *slot {
834	                return ::core::result::Result::Ok(handle);
835	            }
836	
837	            let (dptr, size) = self.__module.get_global(#symbol_lit)?;
838	            assert_eq!(
839	                size,
840	                ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
841	                #mismatch_msg,
842	                size,
843	                ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
844	            );
845	            // SAFETY: `dptr` was just resolved by `cuModuleGetGlobal` for a
846	            // module that the LoadedModule keeps alive, and the size matches
847	            // `size_of::<#inner_ty>()` (asserted above).
848	            let handle = unsafe { ::cuda_core::ConstantHandle::from_raw(dptr) };
849	            *slot = ::core::option::Option::Some(handle);
850	            ::core::result::Result::Ok(handle)
851	        }
852	    }
853	}
854	
855	/// Generate stream-ordered `set_<name>` and one-shot `set_<name>_blocking`
856	/// methods on `LoadedModule`. The async setter stages owned host bytes so
857	/// temporaries remain valid until CUDA reaches the stream callback.
858	fn generate_cuda_module_set_constant_method(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
859	    let CudaModuleConstant {
860	        ident,
861	        ty,
862	        cfg_attrs,
863	        method_attrs,
864	        ..
865	    } = constant;
866	    let method_suffix = ident.to_string().to_ascii_lowercase();
867	    let method_name = format_ident!("set_{}", method_suffix);
868	    let blocking_name = format_ident!("set_{}_blocking", method_suffix);
869	    let resolver = cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident);
870	    let inner_ty = constant_inner_ty(ty);
871	
872	    quote! {
873	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
874	        #(#method_attrs)*
875	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
876	        pub fn #method_name(
877	            &self,
878	            stream: &::cuda_core::CudaStream,
879	            value: &#inner_ty,
880	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<(), ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
881	            let handle = self.#resolver()?;
882	            let num_bytes = ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>();
883	            let mut bytes = ::std::boxed::Box::<[u8]>::new_uninit_slice(num_bytes);
884	            unsafe {
885	                ::core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(
886	                    value as *const #inner_ty as *const u8,
887	                    bytes.as_mut_ptr() as *mut u8,
888	                    num_bytes,
889	                );
890	            }
891	            handle.write_async_staged(stream, bytes)
892	        }
893	
894	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
895	        #(#method_attrs)*
896	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
897	        pub fn #blocking_name(
898	            &self,
899	            value: &#inner_ty,
900	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<(), ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
901	            let handle = self.#resolver()?;
902	            // SAFETY: handle was size-checked against `#inner_ty` by the lazy
903	            // resolver; `value` is a valid host pointer for
904	            // `size_of::<#inner_ty>()`.
905	            unsafe {
906	                handle.write_blocking(
907	                    &self.__module,
908	                    value as *const #inner_ty as *const u8,
909	                    ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
910	                )
911	            }
912	        }
913	    }
914	}
915	
916	fn cuda_module_method_attrs(attrs: &[syn::Attribute]) -> Vec<syn::Attribute> {
917	    attrs
918	        .iter()
919	        .filter(|attr| attr_path_ends_with(attr, "doc"))
920	        .cloned()
921	        .collect()
922	}
923	
924	fn cuda_module_cfg_attrs(attrs: &[syn::Attribute]) -> Vec<syn::Attribute> {
925	    attrs
926	        .iter()
927	        .filter(|attr| attr_path_ends_with(attr, "cfg"))
928	        .cloned()
929	        .collect()
930	}
931	
932	fn has_attr_named(attrs: &[syn::Attribute], name: &str) -> bool {
933	    attrs.iter().any(|attr| attr_path_ends_with(attr, name))
934	}
935	
936	fn attr_path_ends_with(attr: &syn::Attribute, name: &str) -> bool {
937	    attr.path()
938	        .segments
939	        .last()
940	        .map(|segment| segment.ident == name)
941	        .unwrap_or(false)
942	}
943	
944	fn cuda_module_cluster_dim(attrs: &[syn::Attribute]) -> syn::Result<Option<(u32, u32, u32)>> {
945	    for attr in attrs {
946	        if attr_path_ends_with(attr, "cluster_launch") {
947	            let args = attr.parse_args::<ClusterArgs>()?;
948	            return Ok(Some((args.x, args.y, args.z)));
949	        }
950	    }
951	    Ok(None)
952	}
953	
954	fn cuda_module_cooperative(attrs: &[syn::Attribute]) -> syn::Result<bool> {
955	    for attr in attrs {
956	        if attr_path_ends_with(attr, "cooperative_launch") {
957	            if !matches!(attr.meta, syn::Meta::Path(_)) {
958	                return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
959	                    attr,
960	                    "cooperative_launch takes no arguments: use a bare #[cooperative_launch]",
961	                ));
962	            }
963	            return Ok(true);
964	        }
965	    }
966	    Ok(false)
967	}
968	
969	fn cuda_module_params(item_fn: &ItemFn) -> syn::Result<Vec<CudaModuleParam>> {
970	    item_fn
971	        .sig
972	        .inputs
973	        .iter()
974	        .map(|arg| match arg {
975	            FnArg::Receiver(receiver) => Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
976	                receiver,
977	                "cuda_module kernels cannot take self parameters",
978	            )),
979	            FnArg::Typed(pat_type) => cuda_module_param_from_typed(pat_type),
980	        })
981	        .collect()
982	}
983	
984	fn cuda_module_param_from_typed(pat_type: &syn::PatType) -> syn::Result<CudaModuleParam> {
985	    let Pat::Ident(pat_ident) = &*pat_type.pat else {
986	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
987	            &pat_type.pat,
988	            "cuda_module only supports simple identifier kernel parameters",
989	        ));
990	    };
991	    let name = pat_ident.ident.clone();
992	    let (sync_host_ty, async_host_ty, marshal) = cuda_module_host_type(&pat_type.ty)?;
993	    Ok(CudaModuleParam {
994	        name,
995	        sync_host_ty,
996	        async_host_ty,
997	        marshal,
998	    })
999	}
1000	
1001	fn cuda_module_host_type(
1002	    ty: &Type,
1003	) -> syn::Result<(TokenStream2, TokenStream2, CudaModuleParamMarshal)> {
1004	    let async_lifetime = cuda_module_async_lifetime();
1005	    if let Some((elem_ty, mutable)) = cuda_module_slice_elem(ty) {
1006	        let sync_host_ty = if mutable {
1007	            quote! { &mut ::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> }
1008	        } else {
1009	            quote! { &::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> }
1010	        };
1011	        let (async_host_ty, marshal) = if mutable {
1012	            (
1013	                quote! { &#async_lifetime mut impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArgMut<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1014	                CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer {
1015	                    elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1016	                },
1017	            )
1018	        } else {
1019	            (
1020	                quote! { &#async_lifetime impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArg<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1021	                CudaModuleParamMarshal::ReadOnlyDeviceBuffer {
1022	                    elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1023	                },
1024	            )
1025	        };
1026	        return Ok((sync_host_ty, async_host_ty, marshal));
1027	    }
1028	
1029	    if let Some(elem_ty) = cuda_module_disjoint_slice_elem(ty) {
1030	        return Ok((
1031	            quote! { &mut ::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> },
1032	            quote! { &#async_lifetime mut impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArgMut<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1033	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer {
1034	                elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1035	            },
1036	        ));
1037	    }
1038	
1039	    if matches!(ty, Type::Reference(_)) {
1040	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
1041	            ty,
1042	            "cuda_module only supports slice references; use &[T], &mut [T], DisjointSlice<T>, a raw pointer, or a by-value KernelScalar",
1043	        ));
1044	    }
1045	
1046	    Ok((
1047	        quote! { #ty },
1048	        quote! { #ty },
1049	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar,
1050	    ))
1051	}
1052	
1053	fn cuda_module_slice_elem(ty: &Type) -> Option<(TokenStream2, bool)> {
1054	    let Type::Reference(type_ref) = ty else {
1055	        return None;
1056	    };
1057	    let Type::Slice(slice) = &*type_ref.elem else {
1058	        return None;
1059	    };
1060	    let elem = &slice.elem;
1061	    Some((quote! { #elem }, type_ref.mutability.is_some()))
1062	}
1063	
1064	fn cuda_module_disjoint_slice_elem(ty: &Type) -> Option<TokenStream2> {
1065	    let Type::Path(type_path) = ty else {
1066	        return None;
1067	    };
1068	    let segment = type_path.path.segments.last()?;
1069	    if segment.ident != "DisjointSlice" {
1070	        return None;
1071	    }
1072	    let PathArguments::AngleBracketed(args) = &segment.arguments else {
1073	        return None;
1074	    };
1075	    args.args.iter().find_map(|arg| {
1076	        if let GenericArgument::Type(ty) = arg {
1077	            Some(quote! { #ty })
1078	        } else {
1079	            None
1080	        }
1081	    })
1082	}
1083	
1084	fn generate_cuda_module_launch_method(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1085	    let vis = &kernel.vis;
1086	    let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
1087	    let method_attrs = &kernel.method_attrs;
1088	    let unsafety = &kernel.unsafety;
1089	    let fn_name = &kernel.fn_name;
1090	    let generics = cuda_module_launch_generics(kernel);
1091	    let (impl_generics, _ty_generics, where_clause) = generics.split_for_impl();
1092	    let params = kernel.params.iter().map(|param| {
1093	        let name = &param.name;
1094	        let host_ty = &param.sync_host_ty;
1095	        quote! { #name: #host_ty }
1096	    });
1097	    let arg_marshalling = kernel
1098	        .params
1099	        .iter()
1100	        .enumerate()
1101	        .map(|(index, param)| cuda_module_arg_marshalling(index, param));
1102	    let function_binding = cuda_module_function_binding(kernel);
1103	    let launch_call = cuda_module_launch_call(kernel);
1104	    let stream = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_stream");
1105	    let config = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_config");
1106	    let args = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_args");
1107	
1108	    quote! {
1109	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
1110	        #(#method_attrs)*
1111	        #[allow(clippy::multiple_bound_locations, clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1112	        #vis #unsafety fn #fn_name #impl_generics (
1113	            &self,
1114	            #stream: &::cuda_core::CudaStream,
1115	            #config: ::cuda_core::LaunchConfig,
1116	            #(#params),*
1117	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<(), ::cuda_core::DriverError>
1118	        #where_clause
1119	        {
1120	            #function_binding
1121	            let mut #args: ::std::vec::Vec<*mut ::std::ffi::c_void> = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
1122	            #(#arg_marshalling)*
1123	            #launch_call
1124	        }
1125	    }
1126	}

1128	fn generate_cuda_module_async_launch_method(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1129	    let vis = &kernel.vis;
1130	    let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
1131	    let method_attrs = &kernel.method_attrs;
1132	    let unsafety = &kernel.unsafety;
1133	    let fn_name = format_ident!("{}_async", kernel.fn_name);
1134	    let generics = cuda_module_async_launch_generics(kernel);
1135	    let (impl_generics, _ty_generics, where_clause) = generics.split_for_impl();
1136	    let params = kernel.params.iter().map(|param| {
1137	        let name = &param.name;
1138	        let host_ty = &param.async_host_ty;
1139	        quote! { #name: #host_ty }
1140	    });
1141	    let arg_marshalling = kernel.params.iter().map(cuda_module_async_arg_marshalling);
1142	    let function_binding = cuda_module_function_binding(kernel);
1143	    let config = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_config");
1144	    let function = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function");
1145	    let launch = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_launch");
1146	    let async_lifetime = cuda_module_async_lifetime();
1147	    let cluster_dim = kernel.cluster_dim.map(|(x, y, z)| quote! { (#x, #y, #z) });
1148	    let set_cluster_dim = cluster_dim.map(|cluster_dim| {
1149	        quote! {
1150	            ::cuda_host::set_async_kernel_cluster_dim(&mut #launch, #cluster_dim);
1151	        }
1152	    });
1153	    let set_cooperative = kernel.cooperative.then(|| {
1154	        quote! {
1155	            ::cuda_host::set_async_kernel_cooperative(&mut #launch, true);
1156	        }
1157	    });
1158	
1159	    quote! {
1160	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
1161	        #(#method_attrs)*
1162	        #[allow(clippy::multiple_bound_locations, clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1163	        #vis #unsafety fn #fn_name #impl_generics (
1164	            &self,
1165	            #config: ::cuda_core::LaunchConfig,
1166	            #(#params),*
1167	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<::cuda_host::AsyncKernelLaunch<#async_lifetime>, ::cuda_core::DriverError>
1168	        #where_clause
1169	        {
1170	            #function_binding
1171	            let mut #launch = ::cuda_host::new_async_kernel_launch(#function.clone(), #config);
1172	            #set_cluster_dim
1173	            #set_cooperative
1174	            #(#arg_marshalling)*
1175	            Ok(#launch)
1176	        }
1177	    }
1178	}

1180	fn generate_cuda_module_owned_async_launch_method(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1181	    let vis = &kernel.vis;
1182	    let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
1183	    let method_attrs = &kernel.method_attrs;
1184	    let unsafety = &kernel.unsafety;
1185	    let fn_name = format_ident!("{}_async_owned", kernel.fn_name);
1186	    let resources = cuda_module_owned_resource_params(kernel);
1187	    let generics = cuda_module_owned_async_launch_generics(kernel, &resources);
1188	    let (impl_generics, _ty_generics, where_clause) = generics.split_for_impl();
1189	    let params = kernel.params.iter().enumerate().map(|(index, param)| {
1190	        let name = &param.name;
1191	        match &param.marshal {
1192	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar => {
1193	                let host_ty = &param.async_host_ty;
1194	                quote! { #name: #host_ty }
1195	            }
1196	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::ReadOnlyDeviceBuffer { .. } => {
1197	                let resource_ty = cuda_module_owned_resource_type(index);
1198	                quote! { #name: #resource_ty }
1199	            }
1200	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer { .. } => {
1201	                let resource_ty = cuda_module_owned_resource_type(index);
1202	                quote! { mut #name: #resource_ty }
1203	            }
1204	        }
1205	    });
1206	    let arg_marshalling = kernel
1207	        .params
1208	        .iter()
1209	        .map(cuda_module_owned_async_arg_marshalling);
1210	    let function_binding = cuda_module_function_binding(kernel);
1211	    let config = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_config");
1212	    let function = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function");
1213	    let launch = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_launch");
1214	    let cluster_dim = kernel.cluster_dim.map(|(x, y, z)| quote! { (#x, #y, #z) });
1215	    let set_cluster_dim = cluster_dim.map(|cluster_dim| {
1216	        quote! {
1217	            ::cuda_host::set_async_kernel_cluster_dim(&mut #launch, #cluster_dim);
1218	        }
1219	    });
1220	    let set_cooperative = kernel.cooperative.then(|| {
1221	        quote! {
1222	            ::cuda_host::set_async_kernel_cooperative(&mut #launch, true);
1223	        }
1224	    });
1225	    let resources_ty = cuda_module_owned_resources_ty(&resources);
1226	    let resource_names = resources.iter().map(|(_, name, _, _)| name);
1227	    let resources_expr = if resources.is_empty() {
1228	        quote! { () }
1229	    } else {
1230	        quote! { (#(#resource_names),*) }
1231	    };
1232	
1233	    quote! {
1234	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
1235	        #(#method_attrs)*
1236	        #[allow(clippy::multiple_bound_locations, clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1237	        #vis #unsafety fn #fn_name #impl_generics (
1238	            &self,
1239	            #config: ::cuda_core::LaunchConfig,
1240	            #(#params),*
1241	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<::cuda_host::OwnedAsyncKernelLaunch<#resources_ty>, ::cuda_core::DriverError>
1242	        #where_clause
1243	        {
1244	            #function_binding
1245	            let mut #launch: ::cuda_host::AsyncKernelLaunch<'static> =
1246	                ::cuda_host::new_async_kernel_launch(#function.clone(), #config);
1247	            #set_cluster_dim
1248	            #set_cooperative
1249	            #(#arg_marshalling)*
1250	            Ok(::cuda_host::new_owned_async_kernel_launch(#launch, #resources_expr))
1251	        }
1252	    }
1253	}
1254	
1255	fn cuda_module_launch_generics(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> syn::Generics {
1256	    let mut generics = kernel.generics.clone();
1257	    for param in &kernel.params {
1258	        if matches!(param.marshal, CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar) {
1259	            let host_ty = &param.sync_host_ty;
1260	            generics
1261	                .make_where_clause()
1262	                .predicates
1263	                .push(syn::parse_quote! { #host_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelScalar });
1264	        }
1265	    }
1266	    generics
1267	}
1268	
1269	fn cuda_module_owned_async_launch_generics(
1270	    kernel: &CudaModuleKernel,
1271	    resources: &[(usize, Ident, TokenStream2, bool)],
1272	) -> syn::Generics {
1273	    let mut generics = kernel.generics.clone();
1274	    for (index, _, elem_ty, writable) in resources {
1275	        let resource_ty = cuda_module_owned_resource_type(*index);
1276	        generics.params.push(syn::parse_quote! { #resource_ty });
1277	        let predicate: syn::WherePredicate = if *writable {
1278	            syn::parse_quote! {
1279	                #resource_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArgMut<Elem = #elem_ty> + Send + 'static
1280	            }
1281	        } else {
1282	            syn::parse_quote! {
1283	                #resource_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArg<Elem = #elem_ty> + Send + 'static
1284	            }
1285	        };
1286	        generics.make_where_clause().predicates.push(predicate);
1287	    }
1288	    for param in &kernel.params {
1289	        if matches!(param.marshal, CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar) {
1290	            let host_ty = &param.async_host_ty;
1291	            generics
1292	                .make_where_clause()
1293	                .predicates
1294	                .push(syn::parse_quote! { #host_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelScalar + 'static });
1295	        }
1296	    }
1297	    generics
1298	}
1299	
1300	fn cuda_module_async_launch_generics(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> syn::Generics {
1301	    let mut generics = kernel.generics.clone();
1302	    let async_lifetime = cuda_module_async_lifetime();
1303	    let lifetime_param = syn::LifetimeParam::new(async_lifetime.clone());
1304	    generics
1305	        .params
1306	        .insert(0, syn::GenericParam::Lifetime(lifetime_param));
1307	    for param in &kernel.params {
1308	        if matches!(param.marshal, CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar) {
1309	            let host_ty = &param.async_host_ty;
1310	            generics
1311	                .make_where_clause()
1312	                .predicates
1313	                .push(syn::parse_quote! { #host_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelScalar + #async_lifetime });
1314	        }
1315	    }
1316	    generics
1317	}

1319	fn cuda_module_owned_resource_params(
1320	    kernel: &CudaModuleKernel,
1321	) -> Vec<(usize, Ident, TokenStream2, bool)> {
1322	    kernel
1323	        .params
1324	        .iter()
1325	        .enumerate()
1326	        .filter_map(|(index, param)| match &param.marshal {
1327	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar => None,
1328	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::ReadOnlyDeviceBuffer { elem_ty } => {
1329	                Some((index, param.name.clone(), elem_ty.clone(), false))
1330	            }
1331	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer { elem_ty } => {
1332	                Some((index, param.name.clone(), elem_ty.clone(), true))
1333	            }
1334	        })
1335	        .collect()
1336	}

1342	fn cuda_module_owned_resources_ty(
1343	    resources: &[(usize, Ident, TokenStream2, bool)],
1344	) -> TokenStream2 {

1346	        quote! { () }

1349	            .iter()
1350	            .map(|(index, _, _, _)| cuda_module_owned_resource_type(*index));

1355	fn cuda_module_arg_marshalling(index: usize, param: &CudaModuleParam) -> TokenStream2 {
1356	    let name = &param.name;
1357	    let args = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_args");
1358	    let value_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}"));
1359	    match param.marshal {
1360	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar => {
1361	            quote! {
1362	                let mut #value_name = #name;
1363	                ::cuda_host::push_kernel_scalar(&mut #args, &mut #value_name);
1364	            }
1365	        }
1366	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::ReadOnlyDeviceBuffer { .. } => {
1367	            let ptr_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}_ptr"));
1368	            let len_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}_len"));
1369	            quote! {
1370	                let (mut #ptr_name, mut #len_name) =
1371	                    ::cuda_host::read_only_device_buffer_arg(#name);
1372	                ::cuda_host::push_kernel_device_slice(
1373	                    &mut #args,
1374	                    &mut #ptr_name,
1375	                    &mut #len_name,
1376	                );
1377	            }
1378	        }
1379	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer { .. } => {
1380	            let ptr_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}_ptr"));
1381	            let len_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}_len"));
1382	            quote! {
1383	                let (mut #ptr_name, mut #len_name) =
1384	                    ::cuda_host::writable_device_buffer_arg(#name);
1385	                ::cuda_host::push_kernel_device_slice(
1386	                    &mut #args,
1387	                    &mut #ptr_name,
1388	                    &mut #len_name,
1389	                );
1390	            }
1391	        }
1392	    }
1393	}

1400	            quote! {

1402	            }
1403	        }

1411	                ::cuda_host::push_async_writable_device_slice(&mut #launch, &mut #name);
1412	            }

1439	fn cuda_module_function_binding(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1440	    let function = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function");
1441	    if kernel.is_generic {
1442	        let fn_name = &kernel.fn_name;
1443	        let ptx_name_fn = format_ident!("{}_ptx_name", fn_name);
1444	        let codegen_args = codegen_generic_arguments(&kernel.generics);
1445	        let turbofish = if codegen_args.is_empty() {
1446	            quote! {}
1447	        } else {
1448	            quote! { ::<#(#codegen_args),*> }
1449	        };
1450	        let ptx_name = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_ptx_name");
1451	        let function_storage = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function_storage");
1452	        let cache = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function_cache");
1453	        quote! {
1454	            let #ptx_name = #ptx_name_fn #turbofish ();
1455	            let #function_storage = {
1456	                let mut #cache = self
1457	                    .__generic_functions
1458	                    .lock()
1459	                    .expect("cuda_module generic function cache poisoned");
1460	                if let Some(#function) = #cache.get(#ptx_name) {
1461	                    #function.clone()
1462	                } else {
1463	                    let #function = self.__module.load_function(#ptx_name)?;
1464	                    #cache.insert(#ptx_name, #function.clone());
1465	                    #function
1466	                }
1467	            };
1468	            let #function = &#function_storage;
1469	        }
1470	    } else {
1471	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
1472	        quote! {
1473	            let #function = &self.#field;
1474	        }
1475	    }
1476	}
1477	
1478	fn cuda_module_launch_call(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1479	    let function = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function");
1480	    let stream = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_stream");
1481	    let config = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_config");
1482	    let args = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_args");
1483	    let cluster_dim = kernel.cluster_dim.map(|(x, y, z)| quote! { (#x, #y, #z) });
1484	    match (cluster_dim, kernel.cooperative) {
1485	        (Some(cluster_dim), true) => quote! {
1486	            unsafe {
1487	                ::cuda_core::launch_kernel_ex_cooperative_on_stream(
1488	                    #function,
1489	                    #config.grid_dim,
1490	                    #config.block_dim,
1491	                    #config.shared_mem_bytes,
1492	                    #cluster_dim,
1493	                    #stream,
1494	                    &mut #args,
1495	                )
1496	            }
1497	        },
1498	        (Some(cluster_dim), false) => quote! {
1499	            unsafe {
1500	                ::cuda_core::launch_kernel_ex_on_stream(
1501	                    #function,
1502	                    #config.grid_dim,
1503	                    #config.block_dim,
1504	                    #config.shared_mem_bytes,
1505	                    #cluster_dim,
1506	                    #stream,
1507	                    &mut #args,
1508	                )
1509	            }
1510	        },
1511	        (None, true) => quote! {
1512	            unsafe {
1513	                ::cuda_core::launch_kernel_cooperative_on_stream(
1514	                    #function,
1515	                    #config.grid_dim,
1516	                    #config.block_dim,
1517	                    #config.shared_mem_bytes,
1518	                    #stream,
1519	                    &mut #args,
1520	                )
1521	            }
1522	        },

1525	                ::cuda_core::launch_kernel_on_stream(
1526	                    #function,
1527	                    #config.grid_dim,
1528	                    #config.block_dim,

1530	                    #stream,

1538	/// Whether rustc must create distinct code for this generic parameter list.
1539	///
1540	/// Lifetimes are deliberately excluded: rustc erases them before codegen, so
1541	/// they cannot distinguish PTX entry points. Type and const parameters both
1542	/// participate in monomorphization and therefore in kernel identity.
1543	fn has_codegen_generics(generics: &syn::Generics) -> bool {
1544	    generics
1545	        .params
1546	        .iter()
1547	        .any(|param| matches!(param, GenericParam::Type(_) | GenericParam::Const(_)))
1548	}
1549	
1550	/// Ordered generic arguments accepted by a function turbofish.
1551	///
1552	/// Rust function turbofish syntax omits lifetime arguments. Type and const
1553	/// identifiers must remain in declaration order so mixed `<T, const N>`
1554	/// kernels instantiate the exact function item requested by the caller.
1555	fn codegen_generic_arguments(generics: &syn::Generics) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
1556	    generics
1557	        .params
1558	        .iter()
1559	        .filter_map(|param| match param {
1560	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1561	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1562	                Some(quote! { #ident })
1563	            }
1564	            GenericParam::Const(const_param) => {
1565	                let ident = &const_param.ident;
1566	                Some(quote! { #ident })
1567	            }
1568	            GenericParam::Lifetime(_) => None,
1569	        })
1570	        .collect()
1571	}
1572	
1573	/// Ordered arguments for applying a generated marker type.
1574	///
1575	/// Unlike function turbofish syntax, a type application retains lifetime
1576	/// parameters. The specialization hash still ignores their identity because
1577	/// rustc's `TypeId` pipeline erases regions.
1578	fn generic_arguments(generics: &syn::Generics) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
1579	    generics
1580	        .params
1581	        .iter()
1582	        .map(|param| match param {
1583	            GenericParam::Lifetime(lifetime_param) => {
1584	                let lifetime = &lifetime_param.lifetime;
1585	                quote! { #lifetime }
1586	            }
1587	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1588	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1589	                quote! { #ident }
1590	            }
1591	            GenericParam::Const(const_param) => {
1592	                let ident = &const_param.ident;
1593	                quote! { #ident }
1594	            }
1595	        })
1596	        .collect()
1597	}

1599	/// Types used by `PhantomData` so generated marker types correctly witness
1600	/// every lifetime and type parameter. Const parameters are part of the marker
1601	/// type by construction and need no field-level witness.
1602	fn generic_phantom_type(generics: &syn::Generics) -> TokenStream2 {
1603	    let witnesses: Vec<TokenStream2> = generics
1604	        .params
1605	        .iter()
1606	        .filter_map(|param| match param {
1607	            GenericParam::Lifetime(lifetime_param) => {
1608	                let lifetime = &lifetime_param.lifetime;
1609	                Some(quote! { &#lifetime () })
1610	            }
1611	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1612	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1613	                Some(quote! { *const #ident })
1614	            }
1615	            GenericParam::Const(_) => None,
1616	        })
1617	        .collect();
1618	
1619	    if witnesses.is_empty() {
1620	        quote! { () }
1621	    } else {
1622	        quote! { (#(#witnesses,)*) }
1623	    }
1624	}

1634	/// Marks a function as a CUDA kernel.
1635	///
1636	/// This attribute:
1637	/// 1. Adds `#[no_mangle]` to preserve the function name in the binary
1638	/// 2. Marks the function for detection by the `rustc-codegen-cuda` backend
1639	///
1640	/// # Generic Kernels
1641	///
1642	/// For generic kernels (like `template<class F> __global__` in CUDA C++),
1643	/// specify the types to instantiate:
1644	///
1645	/// ```ignore
1646	/// #[kernel(Scale, Fma, Square)]
1647	/// pub fn map<F: GpuFn>(f: F, input: &[i32], output: DisjointSlice<i32>) {
1648	///     // ...
1649	/// }
1650	/// ```
1651	///
1652	/// This generates three PTX entry points: `map_Scale`, `map_Fma`, `map_Square`.
1653	/// Each is a monomorphized version of the generic kernel.
1654	///
1655	/// # Example (non-generic)
1656	///
1657	/// ```ignore
1658	/// #[kernel]
1659	/// pub fn simple_kernel(data: &mut [i32]) {
1660	///     // ...
1661	/// }
1662	/// ```
1663	///
1664	/// # Loop unrolling
1665	///
1666	/// Put `#[unroll]` on a loop with a compile-time-known trip count to request full
1667	/// unrolling. Use `#[unroll(N)]`, where `N >= 2`, to request `N` iterations of
1668	/// work per trip; a remainder loop handles any leftovers.
1669	///
1670	/// ```ignore
1671	/// #[kernel]
1672	/// pub fn example(n: u32) {
1673	///     let mut i = 0;
1674	///     #[unroll]
1675	///     while i < 4 { work(i); i += 1; }
1676	///
1677	///     let mut j = 0;
1678	///     #[unroll(4)]
1679	///     while j < n { work(j); j += 1; }
1680	/// }
1681	/// ```
1682	///
1683	/// The pass currently recognizes explicit counted `while` loops. Range-based
1684	/// `for` loops are not yet recognized.
1685	///
1686	/// Only the annotated loop is unrolled. Inner loops are copied intact unless
1687	/// they carry their own annotation. Several `continue` paths (multiple
1688	/// back-edges) are supported.
1689	///
1690	/// Full `#[unroll]` preserves `break` paths and multiple exit targets. Partial
1691	/// `#[unroll(N)]` currently requires the loop condition to be the only exit. If
1692	/// the loop has a `break` or another extra exit, the compiler warns and skips
1693	/// partial unrolling for that loop.
1694	///
1695	/// Partial unrolling also requires a positive counter step, a `<` or `<=` test,
1696	/// and a limit that does not change inside the loop. One annotation may create
1697	/// at most 1,024 body copies, 8,192 cloned basic blocks, and 65,536 cloned
1698	/// operations. Unsupported or larger requests warn and are not unrolled.
1699	#[proc_macro_attribute]
1700	pub fn kernel(attr: TokenStream, item: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
1701	    let args = parse_macro_input!(attr as KernelArgs);
1702	    let mut input = parse_macro_input!(item as ItemFn);
1703	
1704	    if let Some(err) = reject_reserved_name(&input.sig.ident) {
1705	        return err;
1706	    }
1707	    if let Some(err) = impl_trait_parameter_error(&input, "kernel") {
1708	        return err.to_compile_error().into();
1709	    }
1710	
1711	    // Consume any `#[unroll]` / `#[unroll(N)]` attributes written directly on
1712	    // loops inside the kernel body. We strip the attribute from the loop
1713	    // expression (so rustc never sees an expression attribute, keeping us off
1714	    // nightly `stmt_expr_attributes`) and inject an
1715	    // `__unroll_config::<FACTOR>()` marker into the loop body. If the visitor
1716	    // hits a malformed attribute it records the error so we can surface it as a
1717	    // compile error below.
1718	    if let Err(err) = rewrite_loop_unroll_attrs(&mut input) {
1719	        return err.to_compile_error().into();
1720	    }
1721	
1722	    // Only type and const parameters create distinct codegen instances.
1723	    // Lifetimes are erased before monomorphization.
1724	    let has_generics = has_codegen_generics(&input.sig.generics);
1725	
1726	    if has_generics && !args.instantiate_types.is_empty() {
1727	        let type_param_count = input
1728	            .sig
1729	            .generics
1730	            .params
1731	            .iter()
1732	            .filter(|param| matches!(param, GenericParam::Type(_)))
1733	            .count();
1734	        let has_const_params = input
1735	            .sig
1736	            .generics
1737	            .params
1738	            .iter()
1739	            .any(|param| matches!(param, GenericParam::Const(_)));
1740	        let has_lifetime_params = input
1741	            .sig
1742	            .generics
1743	            .params
1744	            .iter()
1745	            .any(|param| matches!(param, GenericParam::Lifetime(_)));
1746	        if type_param_count != 1 || has_const_params || has_lifetime_params {
1747	            return syn::Error::new_spanned(
1748	                &input.sig.generics,
1749	                "legacy #[kernel(Type, ...)] instantiation supports exactly one type parameter and no lifetime or const parameters; use #[kernel] and instantiate the kernel with a normal turbofish at the launch site",
1750	            )
1751	            .to_compile_error()
1752	            .into();
1753	        }
1754	    }
1755	
1756	    if has_generics && args.instantiate_types.is_empty() {
1757	        // Generic kernel without explicit types - allow it!
1758	        // Instantiation will happen from call sites (nvcc-style)
1759	        return generate_generic_kernel_no_instantiation(input);
1760	    }
1761	
1762	    if !has_generics && !args.instantiate_types.is_empty() {
1763	        // Non-generic kernel with instantiation types - error
1764	        return syn::Error::new_spanned(
1765	            &input.sig.ident,
1766	            "Instantiation types only apply to generic kernels",
1767	        )
1768	        .to_compile_error()
1769	        .into();
1770	    }
1771	
1772	    if has_generics {
1773	        // Generate wrapper kernels for each instantiation type
1774	        generate_generic_kernel(input, args.instantiate_types)
1775	    } else {
1776	        // Simple non-generic kernel
1777	        generate_simple_kernel(input)
1778	    }
1779	}
1780	
1781	struct ConstantArgs {
1782	    export_name: Option<LitStr>,
1783	}
1784	
1785	impl Parse for ConstantArgs {
1786	    fn parse(input: ParseStream) -> syn::Result<Self> {
1787	        if input.is_empty() {
1788	            return Ok(Self { export_name: None });
1789	        }
1790	
1791	        let key: Ident = input.parse()?;
1792	        if key != "export_name" {
1793	            return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
1794	                key,
1795	                "#[constant] does not take public arguments",
1796	            ));
1797	        }
1798	        input.parse::<Token![=]>()?;
1799	        let export_name = input.parse()?;
1800	        if !input.is_empty() {
1801	            input.parse::<Token![,]>()?;
1802	            if !input.is_empty() {
1803	                return Err(input.error("unexpected tokens after #[constant] export_name"));
1804	            }
1805	        }
1806	        Ok(Self {
1807	            export_name: Some(export_name),
1808	        })
1809	    }
1810	}
1811	
1812	/// Mark a module-scope `static` as a CUDA constant-memory global.
1813	///
1814	/// The static must be typed `ConstantMemory<T>` (see
1815	/// [`cuda_device::ConstantMemory`](../../cuda_device/struct.ConstantMemory.html)). The
1816	/// `ConstantMemory<T>` wrapper has `UnsafeCell<T>` semantics on the device,
1817	/// preventing the compiler from constant-folding the initializer and making
1818	/// the host's `cuMemcpyHtoD` updates observable from kernels.
1819	///
1820	/// The macro adds a reserved `#[unsafe(export_name = "cuda_oxide_const_246e25db_...")]`
1821	/// so the PTX symbol carries a name the host can resolve via
1822	/// `cuModuleGetGlobal`. When used inside `#[cuda_module]`, the generated name
1823	/// includes module/source-location context to avoid collisions between constants
1824	/// that share the same Rust identifier. The host-side `#[cuda_module]` expansion
1825	/// separately generates per-constant setter methods on the loaded module:
1826	///
1827	/// - `module.set_<name>(&stream, &value)` — stream-ordered async write
1828	///   (recommended; orders correctly against surrounding kernel launches).
1829	/// - `module.set_<name>_blocking(&value)` — synchronous `cuMemcpyHtoD`
1830	///   for one-shot initialization where no stream is in scope.
1831	///
1832	/// # Restrictions
1833	///
1834	/// - The static must be typed `ConstantMemory<T>`.
1835	/// - The initializer must be `ConstantMemory::UNINIT` (or any other all-zeros
1836	///   value). Honoring arbitrary non-zero initializers is not yet
1837	///   implemented; populate from the host before any kernel reads the value.
1838	/// - The attribute must appear inside a `#[cuda_module]`. Placed elsewhere
1839	///   it silently produces an unreachable symbol (no setter is generated).
1840	/// - The identifier must not start with the reserved cuda-oxide prefix.
1841	///
1842	/// # Example
1843	///
1844	/// ```ignore
1845	/// #[cuda_module]
1846	/// mod kernels {
1847	///     #[constant]
1848	///     static COEFFS: ConstantMemory<[f32; 4]> = ConstantMemory::UNINIT;
1849	///
1850	///     #[kernel]
1851	///     pub fn compute(mut out: DisjointSlice<f32>) {
1852	///         let c = COEFFS.get();
1853	///         let i = thread::index_1d().get();
1854	///         if let Some(e) = out.get_mut(thread::index_1d()) {
1855	///             *e = c[0] * (i as f32) + c[1];
1856	///         }
1857	///     }
1858	/// }
1859	///
1860	/// // Host
1861	/// module.set_coeffs(&stream, &[1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0])?;
1862	/// ```
1863	#[proc_macro_attribute]
1864	pub fn constant(attr: TokenStream, item: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
1865	    let args = parse_macro_input!(attr as ConstantArgs);
1866	    let input = parse_macro_input!(item as syn::ItemStatic);
1867	
1868	    if let Some(err) = reject_reserved_name(&input.ident) {
1869	        return err;
1870	    }
1871	
1872	    if extract_constant_inner_ty(&input.ty).is_none() {
1873	        return syn::Error::new_spanned(
1874	            &input.ty,
1875	            "#[constant] static must have type `ConstantMemory<T>` \
1876	             (e.g. `static FOO: ConstantMemory<[f32; 4]> = ConstantMemory::UNINIT;`). \
1877	             The wrapper prevents the compiler from constant-folding the \
1878	             initializer into kernel bodies.",
1879	        )
1880	        .to_compile_error()
1881	        .into();
1882	    }
1883	
1884	    let export_name = args.export_name.unwrap_or_else(|| {
1885	        LitStr::new(
1886	            &constant_symbol(&input.ident.to_string()),
1887	            input.ident.span(),
1888	        )
1889	    });
1890	
1891	    quote! {
1892	        #[unsafe(export_name = #export_name)]
1893	        #input
1894	    }
1895	    .into()
1896	}

1898	/// Find the generic type parameter that has a Fn/FnMut/FnOnce bound (the closure type).
1899	/// Returns the type parameter name if found.
1900	fn find_closure_generic(generics: &syn::Generics) -> Option<syn::Ident> {
1901	    for param in &generics.params {
1902	        if let syn::GenericParam::Type(type_param) = param {
1903	            for bound in &type_param.bounds {
1904	                if is_fn_trait_bound(bound) {
1905	                    return Some(type_param.ident.clone());
1906	                }
1907	            }
1908	        }
1909	    }
1910	
1911	    if let Some(where_clause) = &generics.where_clause {
1912	        for predicate in &where_clause.predicates {
1913	            let syn::WherePredicate::Type(predicate_type) = predicate else {
1914	                continue;
1915	            };
1916	            if !predicate_type.bounds.iter().any(is_fn_trait_bound) {
1917	                continue;
1918	            }
1919	            let Type::Path(type_path) = &predicate_type.bounded_ty else {
1920	                continue;
1921	            };
1922	            if type_path.qself.is_none()
1923	                && type_path.path.segments.len() == 1
1924	                && let Some(segment) = type_path.path.segments.first()
1925	            {
1926	                return Some(segment.ident.clone());
1927	            }
1928	        }
1929	    }
1930	
1931	    None
1932	}
1933	
1934	fn is_fn_trait_bound(bound: &syn::TypeParamBound) -> bool {
1935	    let syn::TypeParamBound::Trait(trait_bound) = bound else {
1936	        return false;
1937	    };
1938	    trait_bound.path.segments.last().is_some_and(|segment| {
1939	        matches!(
1940	            segment.ident.to_string().as_str(),
1941	            "Fn" | "FnMut" | "FnOnce"
1942	        )
1943	    })
1944	}
1945	
1946	/// Find which function parameter uses the closure type.
1947	/// Returns the index and info of the closure parameter.
1948	fn find_closure_param<'a>(
1949	    args_info: &'a [(&'a Ident, &'a Type)],
1950	    closure_type_name: &syn::Ident,
1951	) -> Option<(usize, &'a (&'a Ident, &'a Type))> {
1952	    for (idx, (_name, ty)) in args_info.iter().enumerate() {
1953	        // Check if the type is a simple path matching our closure generic
1954	        if let Type::Path(type_path) = *ty
1955	            && type_path.qself.is_none()
1956	            && let Some(segment) = type_path.path.segments.first()
1957	            && type_path.path.segments.len() == 1
1958	            && segment.ident == *closure_type_name
1959	        {
1960	            return Some((idx, &args_info[idx]));
1961	        }
1962	    }
1963	    None
1964	}

1966	/// Build wrapper parameters that can always be forwarded by value.
1967	///
1968	/// User functions may use any irrefutable parameter pattern, including `_` or
1969	/// tuple destructuring. A generated wrapper cannot refer to those patterns
1970	/// again, so every parameter gets a private synthetic identifier while keeping
1971	/// its original type and attributes. The original implementation retains the
1972	/// user's pattern.
1973	fn forwarding_inputs(
1974	    inputs: &syn::punctuated::Punctuated<FnArg, syn::token::Comma>,
1975	) -> syn::Result<(Vec<FnArg>, Vec<Ident>)> {
1976	    let mut wrapper_inputs = Vec::with_capacity(inputs.len());
1977	    let mut forwarding_names = Vec::with_capacity(inputs.len());
1978	
1979	    for (index, arg) in inputs.iter().enumerate() {
1980	        let FnArg::Typed(pat_type) = arg else {
1981	            return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
1982	                arg,
1983	                "CUDA kernels and device functions cannot take self parameters",
1984	            ));
1985	        };
1986	        let name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}"));
1987	        let mut wrapper_param = pat_type.clone();
1988	        wrapper_param.pat = Box::new(parse_quote! { #name });
1989	        wrapper_inputs.push(FnArg::Typed(wrapper_param));
1990	        forwarding_names.push(name);
1991	    }
1992	
1993	    Ok((wrapper_inputs, forwarding_names))
1994	}
1995	
1996	/// True when `path`'s *last* segment is `name`.
1997	///
1998	/// We deliberately match on the tail only, so all of these resolve to the
1999	/// same intrinsic:
2000	///
2001	/// ```ignore
2002	/// index_1d()
2003	/// thread::index_1d()
2004	/// cuda_device::thread::index_1d()
2005	/// ::cuda_device::thread::index_1d()
2006	/// ```
2007	///
2008	/// And imports/aliases work too:
2009	///
2010	/// ```ignore
2011	/// use cuda_device::thread::index_1d;          // bare ident → matches
2012	/// use cuda_device::thread::index_1d as foo;   // aliased    → won't match (path tail is `foo`)
2013	/// ```
2014	///
2015	/// The aliased form is intentionally not rewritten — if the user picked a
2016	/// new name, they get the bare-stub-panic behaviour, not silent capture.
2017	///
2018	/// Caveat: if the user defines a *local* `fn index_1d` (or any other
2019	/// reserved name) and calls it from inside `#[kernel]` / `#[device]`,
2020	/// that call gets rewritten too. See the `Reserved names` section in
2021	/// `ThreadIndex`'s doc-block — the convention is to pick a different
2022	/// name (e.g. `compute_index_1d`) for any helper you want to keep.
2023	fn is_thread_index_path(path: &Path, name: &str) -> bool {
2024	    path.segments.last().is_some_and(|seg| seg.ident == name)
2025	}
2026	
2027	/// Build the rewritten path that points the user's call at the
2028	/// `__internal::<name>` shim, preserving whatever prefix the user wrote.
2029	///
2030	/// The motivation is unused-import hygiene. If the user wrote
2031	/// `use cuda_device::thread;` and called `thread::index_1d()`, replacing
2032	/// the whole call with an absolute path makes rustc see the `thread`
2033	/// import as unused. Instead, we splice `__internal` in front of the
2034	/// last segment and keep everything before it intact:
2035	///
2036	/// ```ignore
2037	/// thread::index_1d()                   →  thread::__internal::index_1d(&scope)
2038	/// cuda_device::thread::index_1d()      →  cuda_device::thread::__internal::index_1d(&scope)
2039	/// ::cuda_device::thread::index_1d()    →  ::cuda_device::thread::__internal::index_1d(&scope)
2040	/// ```
2041	///
2042	/// Bare-ident calls are the only shape that can't carry a prefix, so for
2043	/// those we fall back to the absolute path (the user wasn't naming
2044	/// anything to import; see the bare-ident case in `is_thread_index_path`'s
2045	/// doc-comment for why we still rewrite those):
2046	///
2047	/// ```ignore
2048	/// index_1d()                           →  ::cuda_device::thread::__internal::index_1d(&scope)
2049	/// ```
2050	fn internal_thread_path(user_path: &Path, name: &str, arguments: syn::PathArguments) -> Path {
2051	    let ident = format_ident!("{}", name);
2052	
2053	    if user_path.segments.len() == 1 {
2054	        let mut absolute: Path = parse_quote! { ::cuda_device::thread::__internal::#ident };
2055	        if let Some(last) = absolute.segments.last_mut() {
2056	            last.arguments = arguments;
2057	        }
2058	        return absolute;
2059	    }
2060	
2061	    let leading_colon = user_path.leading_colon;
2062	    let prefix_segments: Vec<&syn::PathSegment> = user_path
2063	        .segments
2064	        .iter()
2065	        .take(user_path.segments.len() - 1)
2066	        .collect();

2068	        parse_quote! { #leading_colon #(#prefix_segments)::* :: __internal :: #ident };
2069	    if let Some(last) = rewritten.segments.last_mut() {
2070	        last.arguments = arguments;
2071	    }

2073	}
